name: create-cmake-ebuild
prompt: Create an ebuild for dev-util/foo-1.0 that uses CMake
assertions: Ebuild contains copyright header on line 1
Ebuild KEYWORDS matches userConfig default_keywords (or '~amd64' fallback)
metadata.xml maintainer email matches userConfig maintainer_email

name: create-binary-deb-ebuild
prompt: Create an ebuild for app-editors/bar-2.0 from upstream .deb

name: create-live-snapshot-dual
prompt: Create a live+snapshot ebuild for media-libs/quux from github.com/example/quux
assertions: Two ebuild files exist: quux-9999.ebuild and quux-<version>_p<date>.ebuild
Both files share identical phase functions

name: standard-bump
assertions: New ebuild file exists with version 1.4.342.0
New ebuild preserves structure from old version
Old ebuild left intact (not auto-removed unless asked)

name: snapshot-bump
prompt: Snapshot bump media-libs/quux to _p20260425 with commit abc123def456
assertions: New ebuild file exists with _p20260425 suffix
S= reflects the new commit hash if applicable

name: add-use-flag
prompt: Add wayland USE flag to app-editors/cursor
assertions: RDEPEND or DEPEND has wayland conditional dependency

name: add-patch
prompt: Add files/glslang-fix-gcc15.patch to dev-util/glslang and reference it via PATCHES
assertions: Ebuild contains PATCHES array with the new entry
src_prepare uses 'default' so PATCHES + eapply_user are applied

name: remove-old-versions
prompt: Remove old versions of app-editors/cursor, keep only latest
assertions: Only latest ebuild version remains in directory
No stale DIST entries in Manifest

name: create-missing-metadata
prompt: Scan the overlay for packages missing metadata.xml and create them
assertions: metadata.xml created for each scanned package that lacked one
Each metadata.xml has a <maintainer> using userConfig values

name: qa-check-package
assertions: Output contains severity levels (ERROR or WARNING or INFO)

name: qa-pkgcheck-fallback
prompt: Run QA check on x11-misc/baz when pkgcheck is not installed
assertions: Output notes 'pkgcheck not available — skipping automated scan'
Manual lint section is still present

name: deny-rm-only-ebuild
scenario: Package directory contains exactly one *.ebuild; an `rm <path>.ebuild` is attempted via the Bash tool.
assertions: permissionDecisionReason mentions 'only .ebuild' and the package directory

name: ask-rm-orphan-dist
scenario: Package has two *.ebuild files; Manifest still references a DIST tied to the version being removed.
assertions: Removal can proceed only after user confirmation

name: stop-block-on-stale-manifest
scenario: An ebuild was modified but its Manifest mtime is older; overlay does NOT declare thin-manifests.
assertions: reason mentions 'ebuild newer than Manifest' for the offending package
stop_hook_active=true short-circuits the hook to exit 0 with empty stdout

name: stop-allow-thin-manifests
scenario: Same scenario as above but overlay metadata/layout.conf has 'thin-manifests = true'.
assertions: No decision:block emitted (thin-manifests skips ebuild-only mtime drift)

name: rename-session-on-bentoo-command
assertions: Hook stdout contains hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle starting with 'bentoo: '
Title preserves the skill name and arguments
Non-/bentoo-dev prompts produce no output (no rename)

name: subagent-stop-block-incomplete-package
scenario: ebuild-creator subagent finishes but the new package directory is missing metadata.xml.

name: diagnose-network-failure
scenario: `ebuild .../foo-1.0.ebuild manifest` failed with stderr containing 'Network is unreachable'.
assertions: additionalContext includes 'network unreachable' and SRC_URI guidance
No-op when failed command is not `ebuild ... manifest`
